title: Caramelized Flaky American Anchovy Pie
description: A savory, sugarfree friendly pie featuring caramelized anchovy fillets, caramelized onions, and a buttery flaky crust, perfect for a solo dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). In a large bowl, combine the flour and a pinch of salt. Add the cold butter cubes and, using a pastry cutter or your fingertips, work the butter into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Drizzle the cold water over the flour mixture and stir just until the dough comes together. Form the dough into a disc, wrap in plastic, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Meanwhile, heat the olive oil in a medium skillet over medium heat. Add the anchovy fillets and cook, turning occasionally, until they are lightly browned and have released a fragrant oil, about 4 minutes.
Add the monk fruit sweetener to the skillet, stirring to dissolve. Continue cooking for 2 minutes until the anchovies caramelize and coat the oil.

Remove the anchovies from the skillet and set aside. In the same skillet, add the sliced onions, thyme, oregano, and crushed red pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the onions are soft and golden brown, about 8 minutes.
Return the caramelized anchovies to the skillet and stir to combine with the onions. Remove from heat.
In a small bowl, mix together the shredded cheddar, grated parmesan, and a light sprinkle of black pepper.

On a lightly floured surface, roll out the chilled dough to fit a 9-inch pie dish. Transfer the dough to the dish and trim the edges. Spoon the anchovy‑onion mixture into the crust and sprinkle the cheese blend on top. Cover with a second dough layer, seal the edges, and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Bake the pie for 25‑30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is bubbling. Let the pie rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
Serve the pie warm, preferably with a crisp green salad.
